nodes.name: When Called by Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Expected input: website_url, client_email, client_name, row_number

parameters.errorMessage: =Website unreachable or returned empty content: {{ $('When Called by Orchestrator').item.json.website_url }} (status {{ $json.statusCode }})

nodes.name: Find Internal Links
nodes.notes: Looks for About/Services/Contact pages on the same origin, capped at 2, for richer AI context.

nodes.name: Scrape Internal Pages
nodes.notes: Best-effort enrichment: failures on individual internal pages are swallowed, never block the pipeline.

nodes.name: Generate Site Spec (OpenAI)
nodes.notes: Calls OpenAI Chat Completions directly (not the native node) so the JSON response_format contract is exact and hand-verifiable.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ { model: 'gpt-4o-mini', temperature: 0.7, response_format: { type: 'json_object' }, messages: [ { role: 'system', content: 'You are an expert web designer and copywriter. Given raw scraped content from a business website, produce a complete redesign brief as strict JSON only — no markdown, no commentary, no code fences. JSON shape: { businessName: string, tagline: string, colorPalette: { primary, secondary, accent, background, text } (hex colors), seoDescription: string under 160 characters, sections: array of { type: one of hero, about, services, testimonials, gallery, contact; heading: string; body: string; items: optional array of short strings } }. Sections must include at least hero, about, services, and contact. Use a modern, cohesive color palette. Testimonials must be clearly generic placeholders, not real names or claims.' }, { role: 'user', content: 'Business name guess: ' + $json.businessNameGuess + '
Meta description: ' + $json.metaDescription + '

Scraped website content:
' + $json.aggregatedContent } ] } }}

nodes.name: Parse Site Spec
nodes.notes: Throws (fails the node/execution) on malformed or incomplete AI output rather than deploying a broken site.

nodes.name: Build Site Files (HTML)
nodes.notes: Renders the AI site spec into a static, dependency-free index.html (Tailwind via CDN, no build step needed for GitHub Pages).

nodes.name: Compute Repo Name
nodes.notes: Repo name is deterministic from businessName + row_number, so re-running this workflow for the same row always targets the same repo (idempotent retries).

nodes.name: Create GitHub Repo
nodes.notes: Owner is hardcoded to the team GitHub account (AhmedIrfan7) that hosts every generated site. Change if deploying under a different account/org.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { name: $json.repoName, description: 'Auto-generated preview site for ' + $json.businessName, auto_init: true, private: false } }}

parameters.errorMessage: =GitHub repo creation failed for {{ $('Compute Repo Name').first().json.repoName }} (status {{ $json.statusCode }}): {{ JSON.stringify($json.data) }}

nodes.name: Restore Lead Data
nodes.notes: The GitHub create-repo response overwrote the item JSON; pull the file list and repo name back from Compute Repo Name before splitting into per-file upload items.

nodes.name: Get Existing File SHA
nodes.notes: 404 (file does not exist yet) is expected on first deploy and is not an error.

nodes.name: Collapse After Upload
nodes.notes: Re-syncs the N per-file items back to a single item before enabling Pages (which is a one-time, repo-level action, not per-file).

nodes.name: Enable GitHub Pages
nodes.notes: Assumes the auto-initialized repo's default branch is 'main' (GitHub's default since 2020).

parameters.errorMessage: =Enabling GitHub Pages failed for {{ $('Compute Repo Name').first().json.repoName }} (status {{ $json.statusCode }}): {{ JSON.stringify($json.data) }}

nodes.name: Wait Extra for Pages Build
nodes.notes: Best-effort single retry. If still not built after this, we proceed anyway — the URL is deterministic and Pages usually finishes building within a minute or two either way.

parameters.subject: =Your new website preview is ready — {{ $json.businessName }}
parameters.message:
={{ `

Your new website is ready!

Hi ${ $('When Called by Orchestrator').first().json.client_name || $json.businessName },

We put together a redesigned preview of your website. Take a look:

View your new site

If the button does not work, copy this link into your browser:
${ $json.liveUrl }

` }}

nodes.name: Format Success Output
nodes.notes: Final node of this workflow. Its output is what the orchestrator (Workflow A) reads via the Execute Workflow node to update the sheet.
